Notice Of Motion And Supplemental Renewed Motion To Lift Stay Pursuant To Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. Section 1008(B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-29-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MOLLY A. DESARIO, Esq. of Capstone Law APC (Tel: 310-556-4811) for plaintiffs Syed Shamsuddoha and Miriam Hernandez;
Atty. EVAN R. MOSES, Esq. of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C. (Tel: 213-239-9800) for defendants Autozoners, LLC and Autozone, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS SYED SHAMSUDDOHA and MIRIAM HERNANDEZ's Motion And Supplemental Renewed Motion To Lift Stay Pursuant To Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. Section 1008(B).

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' "supplemental renewed motion to lift stay" is denied. The legal terrain has not shifted since plaintiffs' last effort to lift the stay earlier this year. The case in this court is a private attorney general wage-and-hour action on behalf of AutoZone store managers. Similar actions filed earlier in other courts caused our court to stay this case back in 2016.

Our parties have lately focused on a Central District case: Alvarez v. AutoZone, Inc. Plaintiffs say a recent discovery order shows that Alvarez is limited to "employees who unloaded trucks" - i.e., not store managers. But the order states "that plaintiff is entitled to seek discovery as to all employees who participated in unloading trucks on delivery days, even if the employees did not perform the act of physically removing merchandise from the truck." And defendants cite testimony that such employees include store managers. (Mongioi Dep. 89:21-90:17.)

The parties' requests for judicial notice are granted.

Order signed.
